chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presented with a sudden onset of severe, painless, and massive hematemesis.
history: The patient had a history of chronic gastrointestinal bleeding for several months prior to the acute event. He had no significant past medical history and was not on any regular medications. He was a non-smoker and had a moderate alcohol intake.
exam: Vital signs: BP 90/60 mmHg, HR 120 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination revealed a pale and tachycardic patient with a palpable mass in the upper abdomen.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: Hb 6.2 g/dL, MCV 85 fL, INR 1.2, platelets 150 x 10^9/L. Imaging: CT abdomen showed a large mass in the jejunum with significant bleeding.
correct_diagnosis: Massive jejunal gastrointestinal stromal tumour bleed
differentials: Massive jejunal gastrointestinal stromal tumour bleed
correct_ranking: Massive jejunal gastrointestinal stromal tumour bleed
